elements: horizontal grid representing the n-m plane with lines at integer values -1, 0, 1
9 vertical stems (lines/thin cylinders) representing discrete filter coefficient locations
9 blue spheres capping each stem, with heights matching [1,2,1; 2,4,2; 1,2,1]
vertical axis representing the amplitude b_2,2[n,m] with tick marks at 0, 1, 2, 3, 4
axis labels for n, m, and b_2,2[n,m]
separable 1D component stem plots along the back/left margins: horizontal [1, 2, 1] and vertical [1, 2, 1]^T
semi-transparent multiplication grid lines connecting the 1D marginals to the 2D product stems
continuous 3D surface representing the 2D frequency response H(w_x, w_y) = (1 + cos(w_x))(1 + cos(w_y)) scaled to match

interactions.label: Visualization Mode
interactions.teaches: Demonstrates the relationship between the physical 2D spatial coefficients, their construction via 1D outer-product, and the resulting low-pass spectrum.

demo_steps.title: The 2D Binomial Kernel
demo_steps.narration: This 3D stem plot displays the spatial coefficients of the b_2,2 binomial filter. Notice the smooth, symmetric bell shape peaking at a value of 4 in the center and dropping to 1 at the corners, which acts as a gentle, isotropic blurring operator.

demo_steps.title: Separable Construction
demo_steps.narration: A key efficiency of this filter is separability: the 2D grid is formed by the outer product of a 1D horizontal [1, 2, 1] filter and a 1D vertical [1, 2, 1]^T filter. This allows 2D convolution to be computed much faster as two successive 1D convolutions.

demo_steps.title: Low-Pass Frequency Response
demo_steps.narration: In the frequency domain, the binomial filter forms a smooth low-pass dome. High-frequency corner regions (representing checkerboard noise) are completely zeroed out, explaining why this filter perfectly eliminates the checkerboard noise shown in the text.

interactionPlan.camera_suggestion: An elevated perspective view looking down at the grid, showing both n and m axes clearly without self-occlusion of the central stem.
interactionPlan.view_reasoning: (1) The dominant n-axis runs diagonally down and to the right in the source image, while the m-axis recedes diagonally up and to the left. (2) This relative layout implies the camera is positioned in the front-right octant, looking toward the origin at an azimuth near -45 degrees (or 315 degrees). (3) The vertical lines are clearly visible, and the horizontal plane is seen from a moderate elevation, indicating an elevation tilt of approximately 25 to 30 degrees to balance depth perception and height visibility.

camera_view.view_notes: Maintains the classic DSP textbook 3D stem plot perspective, ensuring that the tall center stem does not completely block the stems directly behind it.

inline_constraints.default_view: matches the cropped 3D stem plot aspect ratio and gray-gridded aesthetic of the original LaTeX output
inline_constraints.visible_ui: at most one compact selector at the top-right corner to transition modes
inline_constraints.explainers: hover tooltips on individual stems show the exact coefficient value (e.g., 'b[0,0] = 4') or frequency coordinate
inline_constraints.framing: keep tight margins with subtle gridlines so the 3D structure pops
inline_constraints.camera: must start at the exact azimuth/elevation of the static figure for a seamless visual swap

interactionPlan.notes: Transition between the discrete stems and the continuous frequency surface using a morphing animation or smooth fade. When in 'Separability' mode, highlight the active multiplying elements on the margins with a distinct color (e.g., light green or orange) to visually map out the outer product calculation.

evaluation.viewing_angle_source: The source figure shows a 3D stem plot viewed from a high elevation, looking down-left. The vertical axis is positioned on the far left, with the n-axis extending to the right and the m-axis extending to the back-left.
evaluation.viewing_angle_rendered: The rendered screenshot shows the 3D stem plot from a high elevation, but the vertical axis is positioned on the far right. The grid orientation places the n-axis label at the bottom and the m-axis label floating near the center-right.
evaluation.viewing_angle_comparison: The viewing angles are rotated about 180 degrees relative to each other. The original has the vertical axis on the left, while the rendered version has it on the right.

shape_comparison.source_shape: flat grid with vertical boundary lines

discrepancies: The camera view is rotated, placing the vertical coordinate axis on the right instead of the left.
The vertical guide/cage lines rising from the grid boundaries are missing in the rendered version.
The label 'm' is floating in the middle of the stems, causing visual clutter and overlap.
The main axis label 'b2,2[n,m]' is missing or not visible in the initial screenshot frame.
The vertical axis ticks include '3' and are equally spaced, whereas the original has non-uniform ticks (0, 1, 2, 4).
evaluation.notes: Excellent conceptual modes (separability and frequency response), but the viewing angle, missing cage lines, and cluttered label placements reduce the visual faithfulness.
action_items: Rotate the camera view so the vertical axis is on the left, matching the original figure's perspective.
Add the thin vertical guide lines rising from the grid boundary corners to match the cage structure of the source.
Move the 'm' label to the outer corner of the grid to avoid overlap with the stems.
Ensure the 'b2,2[n,m]' vertical axis label is visible and aligned with the vertical axis on the left.
Change the vertical axis ticks to 0, 1, 2, 4 to match the original layout.

feedback.rationale: The concept and interactivity are highly accurate, but the implementation needs concrete visual refinements including camera rotation, label placement, custom axis ticks, and adding grid cage lines.
action_items: Rotate the camera view so the vertical axis is on the left, matching the original figure's perspective.

attempts.html: ⚠ Figure failed to load

evaluation.viewing_angle_source: The source figure shows a 3D stem plot from an elevated perspective looking down. The vertical amplitude axis is on the left-back of the grid. The ground axis 'm' extends towards the bottom-left, while the 'n' axis extends towards the bottom-right.
evaluation.viewing_angle_rendered: The rendered screenshot shows the plot from a lower elevation angle. The vertical amplitude axis is positioned on the right-back side of the grid. The 'n' axis extends towards the bottom-center/left, and the camera is extremely zoomed in.
evaluation.viewing_angle_comparison: The viewing angles are significantly different. The rendered camera is rotated such that the vertical axis is on the right instead of the left, the elevation is too low, and the framing is over-zoomed, causing labels to clip at the edges.
discrepancies: The vertical amplitude axis is on the right side of the plot instead of the left side.
The camera zoom is too tight, causing the 'n' label at the bottom to be cut off.
The 'm' and 'n' coordinate axes are swapped/rotated relative to their positions in the source.
The 'b2,2[n,m]' axis label is placed on the right side and is slightly clipped.
evaluation.notes: Excellent conceptual features (separability and frequency response modes), but fails on exact visual faithfulness due to a reversed/rotated camera perspective and clipped labels.
action_items: Reposition the camera and rotate the scene so that the vertical axis with the tick marks appears on the left-back side, matching the source.
Decrease the camera zoom (increase camera frustum or adjust heightFraction) to ensure no labels are cut off at the margins.
Swap or re-orient the ground grid axes so 'm' goes to the bottom-left and 'n' goes to the bottom-right.
Move the 'b2,2[n,m]' label to the left side to match the source positioning.

feedback.rationale: The conceptual model and geometry are accurate (concept accuracy 5, geometry 4), but execution-level adjustments to the camera position, zoom, and axis/label orientation are needed to match the source.
action_items: Reposition the camera and rotate the scene so that the vertical axis with the tick marks appears on the left-back side, matching the source.
Decrease the camera zoom (increase camera frustum or adjust heightFraction) to ensure no labels are cut off at the margins.
Swap or re-orient the ground grid axes so 'm' goes to the bottom-left and 'n' goes to the bottom-right.
Move the 'b2,2[n,m]' label to the left side to match the source positioning.

attempts.html: ⚠ Figure failed to load

evaluation.viewing_angle_source: The source figure is viewed from an elevated, oblique perspective. The 'n' axis extends towards the bottom-right, and the 'm' axis extends towards the bottom-left. The vertical 'b_2,2[n,m]' axis is located on the far-left corner.
evaluation.viewing_angle_rendered: The rendered screenshot is viewed from a similar elevated perspective, but the camera is positioned such that the vertical axis and grid lines are aligned more symmetrically. A vertical axis line runs straight up through the middle of the scene, causing visual overlapping.
evaluation.viewing_angle_comparison: The rendered view is slightly too symmetric compared to the source, and the placement of the vertical coordinate axis in the center rather than on the outer-left boundary deviates from the source's layout.

shape_comparison.source_shape: minimal 2D floor grid with rear vertical guides

discrepancies: The vertical axis is placed in the center of the grid rather than on the left-front edge, causing it to clip through the highest coefficient stem.
The label 'b_2,2[n,m]' is rendered directly on top of the central axis and a stem cap, making it completely unreadable due to overlapping.
The 3D wireframe cage is far too complex and busy compared to the clean, minimalist lines of the original figure.
The axis label text sizes and positions are slightly misaligned.
evaluation.notes: Excellent pedagogical interactivity and accurate concepts, but plagued by severe label overlapping and an overly cluttered 3D wireframe cage.
action_items: Reposition the vertical amplitude axis (b_2,2[n,m]) to the left-front corner, matching the source figure instead of running through the center.
Move the vertical label 'b_2,2[n,m]' further to the left so it does not overlap with any stems, caps, or axes.
Simplify the cage/grid geometry by removing the top and front-right wireframe lines, keeping only the floor grid and rear vertical lines.
Adjust the initial camera angle to more closely match the oblique perspective of the source image.

feedback.rationale: The conceptual structure and interactivity are excellent (rated 5/5), but the execution needs concrete implementation fixes for the camera angle, axis positioning, label overlapping, and wireframe clutter.
